titleInfo.title: Miraculous Retribution: A Study and Translation of T’ang Lin’s Ming-pao chi

mods.abstract: The Ming-pao chi 冥報記 or "Records of Miraculous Retribution," is a collection of some fifty-seven Buddhist miracle tales compiled in the mid-seventh century by a high government official and devout Buddhist layman, Tang Lin 唐臨. Tang Lin lived during one of the most vital periods in Chinese history: a reasonable interpretation of available evidence suggests that he was born in the year 600.... The Ming-pao chi is part of a tradition of Buddhist tale collecting which had begun in China during the Six Dynasties period (220- 589), and which seems to have attracted widest interest and popularity during the Tang dynasty.

name.namePart: Centers for South and Southeast Asia Studies, University of California
